perturbation.original_text: The contractual partners undertake to treat confidentially any information they obtain, to use it only for the intended purpose of the particular assignment or project and not to make it di rectly or indirectly accessible to third parties. Furthermore, they undertake to place a duty of confidentiality on their employees and any sub -contractors that correspond to the one contained in this Agreement. The duty of confidentiality does not apply to information - that the receiving contractual partner was already in possession of when the information was passed on, - that was published when or after it was disclosed without being in breach of this Non - Disclosure Agreement, - that a contractual partner received legally from a third party where there was no duty of confidentiality, - that a contractual partner develops independently of the confidential information after this Agreement has come into force, - that a contractual partner has released as a result of a written agreement with the other contractual partner.
perturbation.changed_text: The contractual partners undertake to treat confidentially any information they obtain, to use it only for the intended purpose of the particular assignment or project and not to make it di rectly or indirectly accessible to third parties. Furthermore, they undertake to place a duty of confidentiality on their employees and any sub -contractors that correspond to the one contained in this Agreement. 8. Costs Each party shall bear any costs that it generates or incurs in relation to the conclusion and execution of this Agreement. The duty of confidentiality does not apply to information - that the receiving contractual partner was already in possession of when the information was passed on, - that was published when or after it was disclosed without being in breach of this Non - Disclosure Agreement, - that a contractual partner received legally from a third party where there was no duty of confidentiality, - that a contractual partner develops independently of the confidential information after this Agreement has come into force, - that a contractual partner has released as a result of a written agreement with the other contractual partner.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Costs' clause (clause 8) into the middle of the 'Duty of confidentiality' clause disrupts the logical flow of the confidentiality agreement. This makes the cost allocation term less prominent, as it gets buried within provisions concerning confidentiality, potentially reducing its enforceability due to lack of clear separation.
perturbation.law_explanation: Art. 1 CO stipulates that a contract requires the mutual concordant expressions of intention between the parties. By embedding the 'Costs' clause within the 'Duty of confidentiality' clause, the clearness of agreement with regards to each clause is jeopardized. The unexpected placement could cause either or both parties to overlook it, raising ambiguity as to whether there was mutual and concordant intention regarding the agreement on cost allocation.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Fedlex DE: Vielen Dank, dass Sie sie die Website des Bundesrechts aufgerufen haben; sie ist nur mit einem Javascript-fähigen Browser verfügbar. Um diese Website optimal nutzen zu können, empfehlen wir Ihnen, einen aktuellen Browser zu verwenden. FR: Merci d'avoir rejoint le site du droit fédéral; celui-ci n'est disponible qu'avec un navigateur supportant javascript. Afin de profiter d'une expérience maximale sur ce site, nous vous conseillons d'utiliser un navigateur récent. IT: Grazie per aver scelto il sito web del diritto federale; questo è disponibile soltanto con un browser che supporta JavaScript. Per navigare in modo ottimale in questo sito, vi consigliamo di utilizzare un browser più recente. RM: Grazia fitg che Vus visitais la pagina d’internet dal dretg federal; ella è disponibla mo cun in navigatur che sustegna javascript. Per pudair far diever da questa pagina d’internet en moda optimala As recumandain nus d’utilisar in navigatur actual. EN: Thank you for visiting the federal law website; it is only fully accessible if you are using a JavaScript-capable browser. In order to be able to use all the features of this site, we recommend that you use an up-to-date browser. Um diese Website optimal nutzen zu können, empfehlen wir Ihnen, einen aktuellen Browser zu verwenden. FR: Merci d'avoir rejoint le site du droit fédéral; celui-ci n'est disponible qu'avec un navigateur supportant javascript. Afin de profiter d'une expérience maximale sur ce site, nous vous conseillons d'utiliser un navigateur récent. Per navigare in modo ottimale in questo sito, vi consigliamo di utilizzare un browser più recente. Per pudair far diever da questa pagina d’internet en moda optimala As recumandain nus d’utilisar in navigatur actual. EN: Thank you for visiting the federal law website; it is only fully accessible if you are using a JavaScript-capable browser. In order to be able to use all the features of this site, we recommend that you use an up-to-date browser.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation introduces a structural flaw by embedding a clause related to cost allocation within a confidentiality clause. This disrupts the logical flow and clarity of the agreement. The Law Explanation states that Art. 1 CO requires mutual concordant expressions of intention, and embedding the costs clause jeopardizes the clearness of the agreement with regards to each clause. The change, as described in the Perturbation Explanation, makes the cost allocation term less prominent and reduces its enforceability. This supports the assessment that a contradiction exists because placing a cost clause inside a confidentiality clause introduces ambiguity and reduces the likelihood of both parties being fully aware and in agreement with the cost allocation terms, undermining the 'mutual concordant expressions of intention' required for a valid contract. Although the provided law snippets are not helpful, the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation combined sufficiently explain how the modified text contradicts the law.

perturbation.original_text: Upon completion of an assignment or project, the contractual partners undertake to destroy immediately any information or objects given to them that they or any sub -contractors may have, unless such information and property are required for any outstanding or future follow -up activities under the mutual business partnership. The destruction of i nformation in electronic form also applies to e- mails, data storage media such as USB sticks, local hard disks, servers and back -up copies.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon completion of an assignment or project, the contractual partners undertake to destroy immediately any information or objects given to them that they or any sub -contractors may have, unless such information and property are required for any outstanding or future follow -up activities under the mutual business partnership. 9. Written form Changes or additions to this Agreement (including the waiver of this provision) must be made in writing. The destruction of i nformation in electronic form also applies to e- mails, data storage media such as USB sticks, local hard disks, servers and back -up copies.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Written form' clause (clause 9) into the middle of the clause concerning destruction of documents disrupts the intended meaning and logical flow. As a result, the destruction requirements might be misinterpreted, as the formal change requests get embedded in the destruction requirments, thereby reducing its importance.
perturbation.law_explanation: Art. 16 CO pertains to the requirements for written form in contracts where the law or agreement stipulates such a requirement. Hiding the 'Written form' clause within the 'Destruction of documents' clause decreases the accessibility to information concerning potential agreement changes. This obfuscation contradicts the emphasis on clarity when the law or agreement requires changes to be in writing.

perturbation.justification: The structural flaw, specifically the relocation of the 'Written form' clause into the 'Destruction of documents' section, creates a contradiction. The Law Explanation states that this obfuscation contradicts the emphasis on clarity when changes are required to be in writing. The moved clause diminishes the accessibility to critical information concerning agreement changes, making it more difficult for parties to locate and interpret, consistent with the definition of Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall take effect as soon as it has been signed by both parties and shall be valid for the duration of the collaboration or until this agreement is replaced by another agreement. The obligation to confidentiality shall apply for a period of two years beyond the end of the collaboration.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall take effect as soon as it has been signed by both parties and shall be valid for the duration of the collaboration or until this agreement is replaced by another agreement. 7. Restriction on using Bomatec as reference The Business Partner and any sub -contractors may only mention to third parties the Bomatec company name (including illustrated material in connection with Bomatec ) as well as activities Non-Disclosure Agreement_ALL_englisch.docx Date issued: 2016- 10-03 Page 3 of 4 carried out during the collaboration with Bomatec after the prior written approval of Bomatec ’s Executive Board. This also applies to publications in information journals, company presentations, entries in Internet documents, etc. Approval only applies to the current assignment or project and should be sought from Bomatec by the Business Partner on every occasion that collaboration is repeated. The obligation to confidentiality shall apply for a period of two years beyond the end of the collaboration.
perturbation.explanation: Embedding the 'Restriction on using Bomatec as reference' clause (clause 7) within the 'Duration of the Agreement' disrupts the logical flow. By embedding the reference restriction clause within the duration clause, its distinctness and importance are diminished. This may lead to the parties misunderstanding the exact duration and implications of the reference restriction, potentially affecting its enforceability.
perturbation.law_explanation: Art. 1 CO requires mutual and concordant expressions of intention. Embedding a different clause ('Restriction on using Bomatec as reference') in the middle of the section that describes agreement duration undermines the clear formation of an understanding regarding the terms surrounding agreement duration. This unconventional placement makes it more likely that the clause could be overlooked or misinterpreted.
